page: Vultr vbm-256c-2048gb-8-mi300x-gpu is a bare metal server configured with dual AMD EPYC 9534 processors. This setup delivers 128 physical cores and 256 vCPUs operating at a base frequency of 2.45 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 2048.0 GB of system memory, providing an allocation of 8.0 GB of RAM per core to support memory-intensive operations.

The primary accelerator architecture consists of eight AMD CDNA3 MI300X GPUs, with each GPU containing 192 GiB of VRAM, totaling 1536 GB of high-bandwidth graphics memory. Local storage is provided by 28608 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, which accommodates large datasets directly on the physical machine. The server also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

This bare metal configuration offers high resource density by combining massive GPU memory capacity with high-core-count AMD EPYC processors on dedicated hardware. The absence of virtualization overhead maximizes raw hardware throughput, though the lack of virtualized scaling options represents a trade-off in flexibility. The hardware profile is designed for large-scale artificial intelligence workloads, deep learning model training, large language model inference, and complex data science computations requiring substantial parallel processing power and local storage capacity.
description: The Vultr vbm-256c-2048gb-8-mi300x-gpu is a bare metal server featuring 256 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 9534 processors running at 2.45 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 2048.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Acceleration is driven by eight AMD CDNA3 MI300X GPUs, delivering a total of 1536 GB of VRAM. For local storage, the server includes 28608 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. This dedicated hardware configuration eliminates virtualization overhead, offering high resource density for parallel computing tasks. The server is designed for large-scale artificial intelligence workloads, deep learning model training, large language model inference, and data-intensive scientific simulations requiring massive GPU memory and high-speed local storage.
